heading: Low-income housing conservation and efficiency grant programs
label: 12807 Low-income housing conservation and efficiency grant programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make available a model program to provide safe, energy-efficient affordable housing for low-income persons.

children.label: A
children.text: owned and occupied by low-income families who have received, are currently receiving, or are scheduled to receive assistance under the weatherization assistance for low-income persons program under part A of title IV of the Energy Conservation and Production Act [ et seq.] (or a comparable Federal or State program);42 U.S.C. 6861

children.label: B
children.text: in danger of becoming uninhabitable within a 5-year period because of structural weaknesses or problems; and

children.label: C
children.text: not sufficiently sound to permit energy conservation improvements without other repair or rehabilitation measures to protect such energy investments;

repairs that will significantly prolong the habitability of units identified under paragraph (1), including roofing, electrical, plumbing, furnace, and foundation repairs or replacement that will prolong the use of the unit as a safe and energy-efficient residence for low-income persons; and
reasonable steps to ensure that any units so repaired will remain occupied by persons or families eligible for assistance under this subchapter.
